codeReview.summary: Implementation covers all expected behavior: bridge MCP to LSP via mcp-language-server, rust-analyzer support (definitions, references, hover, diagnostics), clangd support (C/C++), symbol rename, and workspace diagnostics. 7 LSP tools implemented (LspDefinitionTool, LspReferencesTool, LspHoverTool, LspDiagnosticsTool, LspRenameTool, LspWorkspaceDiagnosticsTool, LspDocumentSymbolsTool). 24 unit tests passing. Code is idiomatic Rust with proper async_trait usage, Arc<dyn Tool> pattern, and serde for JSON serialization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker made a minor change to hooks.rs (line 279) that was not part of the mcp-lsp-integration feature. Changed `config_path: config_path,` to `config_path,` (field init shorthand). This is a legitimate Rust idiom but is unrelated to the feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git diff shows only hooks.rs was modified in working tree, unrelated to MCP-LSP feature

summary: Feature implementation is solid. All 7 LSP tools properly integrate with MCP client infrastructure, tests pass (24 for mcp_lsp, 390 total in swell-tools), and code follows project conventions. No blocking issues found. Minor observation: a tangential change to hooks.rs was made but is not related to this feature.
